subject: A human face reflected in a cracked mirror, with greenish tendrils or worms emerging from the eye sockets and mouth; a hand reaches toward the mirror from the lower-left, while segmented spined appendages frame both sides of the reflection
description: A close-up of a cracked mirror dominates the frame. Within the fractured glass, a human face stares back, illuminated in sickly yellow-green light against deep black surroundings. Wormlike or tendril-like growths spill from the figure's eye sockets and trail across the lips, suggesting parasitic infestation. The mirror is shattered with radiating crack lines and prismatic refractions catching the light. From the lower-left edge, a pale hand with thumb extended reaches up toward the surface as if to touch or steady the mirror. Flanking the reflection on both left and right, segmented chitinous appendages with regular ridged spines curve inward, framing the face like millipede legs or insectoid mandibles. The overall palette is jaundice yellow-green against near-black, with the mirror cracks adding faint rainbow shimmer.
foreground: pale hand reaching from lower-left; segmented spined appendages framing both sides of the mirror
background: shattered mirror surface with cracked glass and prismatic light refractions; sickly green-yellow reflected face behind the glass
emotion: vacant horrified stare; mouth slightly parted as if mid-utterance
